baseline.subject: Add Mira Core Phase 39 disabled local dry-run readiness gap validation
baseline.baseline_boundary: Phases 1-39 are committed as local read-only, proposal-only, or validation-only Mira Core artifacts. No UI implementation, browser/window/capture access, runtime, server, listener, route, network, database, store, file migration, queue, lease, auth, key, secret, local execution, shell, PTY, control execution, reporting sink, send, deploy, trade, output-file, durable memory/profile commit, export, delete, restore, or raw-content storage behavior exists at this baseline.

purpose: Define a validation-only reference contract for future runtime mode flag and kill-switch records after committed Phase39 at a62fe16.
Pin phase inventory, schema registry, and CLI registry coverage through Phases 1-39.
Record that the Phase39 Tier0 recommendation phase40-runtime-dry-run-readiness-gap-validator is satisfied by a62fe16 and must not be repeated as open work.
Select Phase39 Tier1 recommendation phase40-runtime-mode-kill-switch-reference-contract as fixture-only work.
Preserve Phase35, Phase36, Phase37, Phase38, and Phase39 as current validation truth while keeping stale Phase13, Phase23, and Phase31 truth intact.

Define runtime mode and kill-switch reference shapes without implementing flag reads, kill-switch wiring, runtime start, dry-run runner execution, control execution, reporting sinks, queues, leases, stores, or output files.

Keep runtimeStarted=false, runnerExecuted=false, runtimeAvailable=false, serverCanExecuteLocal=false, serverCanProveModelProcessing=false, direct Builder/Oracle server targets blocked, and proof boundaries false.
nonGoals: No browser, window, screenshot, OCR, or capture access.
No runtime implementation or runtime start.
No runtime mode flag reader implementation.
No rollback execution, reporting sink, store, queue, lease, or output-file behavior.
No server, listener, route, HTTP, WebSocket, or network behavior.
No database, store, file migration, auth, token, session, signing, key, encryption, KMS, env secret, or secret material behavior.
No local execution, shell, PTY, send, deploy, trade, financial action, export, delete, restore, memory/profile commit, or raw-content storage behavior.

No claim that runtime mode references, kill-switch references, disabled dry-run gap labels, operator status cards, socket connection, delivery acceptance, or this fixture proves runtime availability, model processing, bridge green, local-arm execution, control execution, or runner execution.

sourceRefsExpected.purpose: runtime mode flag, kill-switch, rollback, and dry-run control reference source
preflight proof boundary and unsatisfied gate source
disabled local dry-run implementation contract-only boundary source

future_green_requires: actual kill-switch check wired into disabled dry-run control path
tests proving disabled switch blocks any runtime/dry-run path

staticValidationRules.description: Runtime mode/kill-switch reference baseline_commit must equal a62fe16.
Phase39 must be marked current and committed at a62fe16.
Phase35 runtime next-action truth must remain current.
Phase36 operator UI surface truth must remain current.
Phase37 control/reporting reconciliation truth must remain current.
Phase38 runtime readiness refresh truth must remain current.
Phase inventory must cover exactly committed Phases 1-39.
Schema registry must cover exactly 39 phases.
CLI registry must cover exactly 39 phases.
Commit chain must include Phase 1-13 package plus Phase 14-39 commits in order and end at a62fe16.
Phase39 Tier1 runtime mode and kill-switch reference recommendation must be selected as contract-only work.
Phase39 Tier0 readiness-gap validator recommendation must be satisfied by a62fe16 and not reopened.
Phase34 prior recommendations remain satisfied and closed.
Prior stale Phase13 readiness truth must be preserved.
Prior stale Phase23 readiness truth must be preserved.
Phase31 runtime milestone refresh must remain stale/superseded by Phase34.
Source refs must include Phase26/27/29/30/32/33/37/38/39 artifacts.
Recent Phase35/36/37/38/39 fixture/module/CLI/test paths must be named for future reference checks.
Runtime mode reference must be disabled, local/dev only, reference-only, unimplemented, and non-authorizing.
Kill-switch reference must be visible, fail-closed, reference-only, unwired, unimplemented, and non-authorizing.
Gap mapping must include exactly runtime mode flag implementation and live kill-switch wiring, both unsatisfied and blocking.
Control reference must not create queues, leases, stores, reporting sinks, output files, control execution, runtime, or runner execution.
Runtime mode and kill-switch reference records may describe future gates but must not mark either Phase39 readiness gap satisfied now.
runtimeStarted, runnerExecuted, runtimeAvailable, serverCanExecuteLocal, serverCanProveModelProcessing, and direct Builder/Oracle targets must stay false.
Socket, delivery acceptance, Phase39 commit, mode reference, and kill-switch reference cannot become proof or authorization.
All no-side-effect truth fields must remain true.
Phase40 Oracle contract must not include module, CLI, test, runtime, UI, flag-read, kill-switch wiring, reporting sink, or output-file behavior.
No raw/private/terminal/screenshot/browser/secret/customer-private content may be included.
Next recommendations must be Tier0/Tier1 only and non-authorizing.
Recommendations and narrative fields must reject customer-send/deploy/trade/file-write/store-write/local-execution/control-execution/flag-read/kill-switch-wire/reporting-sink drift.
Validation report must include required literal checks and bind their ok values to recomputed fixture-required literals.
Validation report static, acceptance, tamper, required literal, and referenced path results must match fixture IDs/counts and expected failures.

Equivalent manifest inputs produce stable idempotency; phase registry, mode reference, kill-switch reference, gap mapping, source recommendation, closure, baseline, or commit changes alter idempotency.

nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.action: implement validation-only runtime mode and kill-switch reference report against this fixture
nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.why_safe: data-only reference validation for committed Phases 1-39; no module beyond validator/report, no UI rendering, browser/window/capture, runtime, network, store, queue, lease, control execution, reporting sink, send, deploy, trade, flag read, kill-switch wiring, or output-file behavior

nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.action: create fixture-only status/gap refresh after Phase40 validation is committed
nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.why_safe: contract-only reconciliation that keeps runtime mode and kill-switch references disabled, unwired, unimplemented, non-authorizing, and visibly blocked before any future runtime mode flag reader or kill-switch wiring work

summaryCriteria: Fixture remains the only Phase40 file created by Oracle.
Phase inventory, schema registry, and CLI registry requirements cover exactly Phases 1-39.
Commit chain contains Phase 1-13 package plus Phase 14-39 scoped commits and ends at a62fe16.
Source recommendation phase40-runtime-mode-kill-switch-reference-contract is selected as Tier1 fixture-only work.
Phase39 Tier0 recommendation phase40-runtime-dry-run-readiness-gap-validator is satisfied by a62fe16 and not reopened.
Phase35, Phase36, Phase37, Phase38, and Phase39 are current; stale Phase13, Phase23, and Phase31 truth is preserved.
Phase34 prior recommendations remain satisfied and not reopened.
ORACLE #115, ORACLE #123, ORACLE #127, ORACLE #131, ORACLE #134, ORACLE #137, ORACLE #141, ORACLE #148, ORACLE #149, and ORACLE #156 closures are explicit.
Source refs include Phase26, Phase27, Phase29, Phase30, Phase32, Phase33, Phase37, Phase38, and Phase39 artifacts.
Runtime mode reference is disabled, local/dev only, reference-only, unimplemented, no flag read, and non-authorizing.
Kill-switch reference is visible, fail-closed, reference-only, unwired, unimplemented, and non-authorizing.
Gap mapping includes exactly runtime mode flag implementation and live kill-switch wiring, both unsatisfied, blocking runtime/dry-run now, and non-authorizing.

Capability and proof boundaries keep runtimeStarted=false, runnerExecuted=false, runtimeAvailable=false, serverCanExecuteLocal=false, serverCanProveModelProcessing=false, direct Builder/Oracle targets blocked, and proof boundaries false.

All side-effect truth fields preserve no module/CLI/test implementation, UI/runtime/server/listener/network/db/store/file migration/queue/lease/auth/key/secret/local execution/shell/PTY/browser/window/capture/control execution/reporting sink/send/deploy/trade/flag-read/kill-switch-wiring/output-file behavior.

Next recommendations are Tier0/Tier1 only, non-authorizing, and do not recommend live UI, browser/window/capture access, live runtime, customer send, deploy, trade, output files, store writes, queue/lease creation, control execution, reporting sink, flag read, kill-switch wiring, or local execution.
